weakest_questions.distractor: It illustrates that only government organisations and national research institutes can afford to train foundation models, meaning all commercial Artificial Intelligence products must build their own from scratch.
weakest_questions.note: Wrong but not a common beginner misconception — the corpus clearly names large technology companies as trainers. Weakest distractor in the set but not absurd; does not breach the 0.25 absurd_rate threshold.

distractor_plausibility.note: Expanded distractors remain plausible beginner misconceptions. Lengthening did not introduce absurdity — distractors describe factually incorrect but structurally coherent claims about the topic.

coverage_map.lo_text: Define what a foundation model is and explain how it differs from the narrow, task-specific AI systems that came before it.
Explain what 'trained once at scale' means and why the scale of training matters.
Describe what 'usable for many tasks' means, and give two examples of different tasks the same foundation model can perform.
Identify at least three real-world foundation models by name and describe the type of input each one handles.
Explain the general idea of adaptation — that a foundation model can be adjusted or guided for a specific use — without confusing adaptation with the original training process.

objective_coverage.note: No questions changed between cycles — only distractor text lengths were adjusted. LO coverage unchanged and intact.

notes: All stems are plain language and self-contained. 'Artificial Intelligence' spelled out in full in all stems.
q3 distractor mentions 'fine-tuning'; the stem explicitly states no retraining occurred, making the distractor self-defeating on its face without requiring the learner to know fine-tuning mechanics.
q4 models (Whisper, GPT-4o, Llama) are all named and characterised in corpus §7. On-scope.
q5 uses 'adaptation through prompting'; prompting is defined in corpus §5.4 and the scenario describes the act explicitly. Answerable from the corpus alone.
No question requires knowledge from topic 4.2 (fine-tuning) or 4.3 (RAG) to identify the correct answer.
Expanded distractor text in q1–q4 introduces no new jargon or out-of-scope concepts.

verdict: Pass — length-bias revision succeeded (longest_correct_rate 0.80 → 0.00, well below 0.40 threshold); positional bias at threshold (max_share=0.40, passes strict >0.40 rule); 0 absurd distractors across 15 (absurd_rate=0.00); all 5 LOs covered 1-to-1 with no orphan questions; no out-of-scope questions or undefined jargon in stems.
